name: Guadeloupe (Région et Département de la Guadeloupe / French DOM-ROM)
summary: Guadeloupe is a French Département d'Outre-Mer / Région d'Outre-Mer (DOM-ROM) operating French civil-law family-law framework under Code civil arts. 371-1 to 387ter (autorité parentale). Family courts operate under the Loi du 27 juin 2018 instituant le juge aux affaires familiales (JAF) framework with French civil-law procedural framework via Tribunal Judiciaire de Pointe-à-Pitre and Tribunal Judiciaire de Basse-Terre. The statutory framework is silent on the 'aliénation parentale' label. The Cour d'appel de Basse-Terre and Cour de cassation develop intérêt supérieur de l'enfant jurisprudence within French DOM framework. EU Brussels IIb framework applies — operationally significant for cross-border family-law adjudication including expatriate-population and metropolitan-France diaspora cases. Substantively significant Caribbean French overseas department framework.

statutory_framework.title: Autorité parentale (Code civil français applicable au DOM)
statutory_framework.relevance: Primary statutory basis for parental authority — French Code civil arts. 371-1 et seq. applicable to Guadeloupe as French DOM. Establishes autorité parentale conjointe (joint parental authority) framework.

statutory_framework.title: Loi instituant le juge aux affaires familiales (applicable au DOM)
statutory_framework.relevance: Loi du 27 juin 2018 establishing juge aux affaires familiales (JAF) framework applicable to Guadeloupe Tribunal Judiciaire framework.

statutory_framework.title: EU cross-border family-law coordination
statutory_framework.relevance: EU Regulation 2019/1111 Brussels IIb effective 1 August 2022 coordinating cross-border family-law matters — operationally significant for Guadeloupe's substantial metropolitan-France diaspora and Caribbean cross-border family-law caseload.

statutory_framework.title: Loi visant à agir contre les violences au sein de la famille
statutory_framework.relevance: Loi Pradié applicable to Guadeloupe as French DOM — strengthens ordonnance de protection regime and limits contact orders where DV is established.

statutory_framework.title: Council of Europe Convention on preventing and combating violence against women and domestic violence
statutory_framework.relevance: Ratified by France 4 July 2014, applicable to Guadeloupe as French DOM.

apex_courts.name: Cour de cassation (chambre civile 1ère)
apex_courts.role: Apex court for civil matters including family law (applicable to Guadeloupe as French DOM)

apex_courts.name: Cour d'appel de Basse-Terre
apex_courts.role: Intermediate appellate court for Guadeloupe family-law matters

apex_courts.name: Tribunal Judiciaire de Pointe-à-Pitre + Tribunal Judiciaire de Basse-Terre
apex_courts.role: First-instance family courts including JAF framework

key_developments.title: French colonisation + Compagnie des Îles d'Amérique framework
key_developments.description: French colonisation of Guadeloupe 1635 under Compagnie des Îles d'Amérique. Substantively foundational French Caribbean civil-law framework — operational basis for subsequent 17th-21st century family-law framework establishment.

key_developments.title: Abolition of slavery + Schoelcher Decree framework
key_developments.description: Abolition of slavery in French colonies 27 April 1848 (Schoelcher Decree). Substantively foundational post-slavery civil-law framework reform — establishing trajectory toward French citizenship extension and full civil-law incorporation.

key_developments.title: Guadeloupe DOM status + French Constitution 1946
key_developments.description: Guadeloupe became French Département d'Outre-Mer 19 March 1946 under French Constitution 1946. Substantively foundational DOM status framework — establishing trajectory toward full French civil-law framework applicability.

key_developments.title: Decentralisation laws + Region creation framework
key_developments.description: Decentralisation laws 1982 (Loi Defferre) creating Guadeloupe Region — substantively significant institutional reform establishing dual department + region framework.

key_developments.title: Loi du 22 décembre 1993 — autorité parentale conjointe framework
key_developments.description: Loi du 22 décembre 1993 applicable to Guadeloupe as French DOM establishing autorité parentale conjointe framework. Substantively significant gender-equal parental-rights reform.

key_developments.title: Loi n° 2002-305 + autorité parentale comprehensive reform
key_developments.description: Loi n° 2002-305 du 4 mars 2002 applicable to Guadeloupe as French DOM comprehensively restructuring Code civil parental-authority framework. Substantively foundational current parental-authority framework.

key_developments.title: 2009 General Strike + LKP framework + social-context framework
key_developments.description: Guadeloupe 2009 General Strike (LKP — Liyannaj Kont Pwofitasyon) January-March 2009 — substantively distinctive Caribbean post-colonial social-context framework affecting institutional context. Subsequent reform agreements substantively affecting subsequent constitutional-democratic-trajectory.

key_developments.title: Loi du 27 juin 2018 — JAF framework applicable to DOM
key_developments.description: Loi du 27 juin 2018 establishing JAF framework applicable to Guadeloupe Tribunal Judiciaire framework. Substantively significant institutional reform consolidating French DOM family-law procedural framework.

key_developments.title: Loi Pradié 2019 + ordonnance de protection framework + Brussels IIb
key_developments.description: Loi Pradié 2019 applicable to Guadeloupe as French DOM. EU Regulation 2019/1111 Brussels IIb subsequently effective 1 August 2022. Substantively significant for DV-PA-allegation intersection cases and cross-border family-law adjudication.

key_developments.title: Continuing social-tensions framework + Caribbean integration framework + AFD framework
key_developments.description: Continuing post-2009 social-tensions framework through 2022. Caribbean integration framework substantively significant for cross-border family-law adjudication with neighbouring Anglophone Caribbean states. Agence Française de Développement (AFD) framework substantively significant institutional context.

key_developments.title: Cour d'appel de Basse-Terre + Cour de cassation + JAF — intérêt supérieur de l'enfant substantive register
key_developments.description: Cour d'appel de Basse-Terre, Cour de cassation, and JAF continue to develop intérêt supérieur de l'enfant jurisprudence under Code civil + Loi 2018 + Brussels IIb framework in custody disputes within French DOM framework. Substantive analysis without doctrinal 'aliénation parentale' label adoption — consistent with metropolitan France HAS non-validation + Défenseure des droits institutional-critique register.

structural_findings: Guadeloupe operates structurally as French DOM-ROM civil-law family-law framework — French Code civil + Loi 2018 JAF + EU Brussels IIb applicable as French DOM — with substantive intérêt supérieur de l'enfant analysis consistent with metropolitan France institutional-critique register without doctrinal 'aliénation parentale' label adoption.

Guadeloupe's substantial metropolitan-France diaspora and Caribbean cross-border framework operationally creates distinctive cross-border family-law adjudication context — substantively significant for cross-border family-law adjudication including expatriate-population cases.

Caribbean DOM framework (alongside Martinique, French Guiana, Mayotte, Réunion) operationally creates structurally distinctive French overseas department family-law adjudication framework — substantively distinguishing DOM framework from COM (collectivité d'outre-mer) framework (New Caledonia, French Polynesia, Wallis and Futuna).
editorial_notes: Guadeloupe's French DOM status operationally creates full French civil-law framework applicability — substantively distinguishing DOM framework from COM framework where local-law adaptation competence may apply.

Cross-border family-law adjudication with neighbouring Anglophone Caribbean states substantively significant institutional context — making Guadeloupe a critical French-Caribbean comparative-law data point.
